28.reason: [When p->left is NULL, calling search(p->left, height+1) leads to dereferencing a null pointer, causing runtime error (segmentation fault). Similarly for p->right.]

29.reason: [Same issue: passing NULL to search and then accessing p->left/p->right in the next call causes undefined behavior.]

41.reason: [If the input n is 0, root remains NULL, and calling search(root, h) would dereference a null pointer. However, the problem states that n integers are read, so n >= 1 likely. But the immediate crash comes from the recursive calls on NULL children.]
